retry_reason: The only failure mode is memory allocation failure; retrying without changed conditions would likely fail again and the function does not provide retry logic.
contract_summary: alua_alloc_pg is a get-or-create wrapper for an alua_port_group identified by device ID and group_id. It returns a pointer to a port group with an additional reference held: either by creating a new object (refcount set to 1 via kref_init) or by acquiring a reference on an existing object via alua_find_get_pg. The caller is responsible for releasing this reference with a corresponding put (e.g., kref_put with the appropriate release function) after use. On memory allocation failure, the function returns ERR_PTR(-ENOMEM); otherwise it always returns a valid pointer with a reference. The function internally synchronizes access to the global port_group list with port_group_lock, but no external locks are required from the caller.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is get-or-create an alua_port_group object and return it with a reference held; all initialization is part of creating the new object, and the refcount management (kref_init or alua_find_get_pg) is central to the function's lifecycle semantics.

check.reason: Both operations are supported: kref_init sets pg->kref.refcount.refs on the newly allocated pg which is returned when no duplicate found; alua_find_get_pg gets tmp_pg->kref.refcount on the existing port group returned as tmp_pg. Access paths and type chains are consistent with the code and callee information.
